html:
Exclusive to:

Pierrot Fish are a Fish Food Item introduced in the Shipwrecked DLC. They can be obtained by stocking Fish Farms with Roe, together with Purple Groupers, Neon Quattro Fish and Tropical Fish. They can be used in the Crock Pot, and count as 0.5 units of Meat and 1 unit of Fish. They are specifically required to cook the Tropical Bouillabaisse. Pierrot Fish cannot be dried using a Drying Rack.

When consumed, Pierrot Fish grants the player a speed increase of 1 and greatly accelerates the rate at which their wetness decreases for 30 seconds. The speed bonus will stack with the bonus from Purple Groupers and other sources, but will not stack with the bonus from Tropical Bouillabaisse.

Cooked Pierrot Fish

A Cooked Pierrot Fish is a Pierrot Fish that has been cooked over a Campfire, Fire Pit, Chiminea or Star. It can also be used in various Crock Pot recipes. Cooked Pierrot Fish grants the same buffs as the uncooked variant.

Usage

Trivia

  • Pierrot Fish were added to the Shipwrecked DLC more than a year after its official completion in the Home Sea Home update, after being tested in a beta branch for several months.
  • The name is a reference to Pierrot, a stock character in commedia dell'arte - essentially a clown. Most likely the appearance is based on a clownfish.

Gallery

  • Walani standing next to a Pierrot Fish and a Cooked Pierrot Fish on the ground.
  • A Fish Farm stocking Pierrot Fish, visible from the yellow fish on the small sign.
  • Pierrot Fish as seen in the poster for the Home Sea Home update.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

“This one's extra water repellent.”
–Wilson, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“Found you!”
–Willow, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“Little fish is very dry!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“The stripes symbolize its emotional imprisonment.”
–Wendy, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“IT LIVES SUBMERGED IN WATER. TERRIFYING”
–WX-78, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“Hmm... I can't seem to identify this species.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“Always did like fishing.”
–Woodie, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“Delightfully pinstriped.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“I predict disastrous consequences if I eat raw fish.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“I wish this meat did nöt have such large eyes.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“It's slippery!”
–Webber, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“I can't kill that! Look how cute it is!”
–Walani, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“How would you like to be a nice chowder?”
–Warly, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“Don't ye b'starin' at ol'Woodlegs!”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“'TIS A STRIPY FISHY”
–Wilba, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“Glub Glub”
–Wormwood, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
“It's a fish. Apparently they live in the water.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Pierrot Fish.
Pierrot Fish are a Fish Food Item introduced in the Shipwrecked DLC. They can be obtained by stocking Fish Farms with Roe, together with Purple Groupers, Neon Quattro Fish and Tropical Fish. They can be used in the Crock Pot, and count as 0.5 units of Meat and 1 unit of Fish. They are specifically required to cook the Tropical Bouillabaisse. Pierrot Fish cannot be dried using a Drying Rack.
When consumed, Pierrot Fish grants the player a speed increase of 1 and greatly accelerates the rate at which their wetness decreases for 30 seconds. The speed bonus will stack with the bonus from Purple Groupers and other sources, but will not stack with the bonus from Tropical Bouillabaisse.

“Gilled to perfection.”
–Wilson, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“I burned it just right.”
–Willow, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“Little fish will make Wolfgang mighty!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“Don't lose your head.”
–Wendy, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“I WILL EAT THIS HORRIBLE WATER-DWELLER”
–WX-78, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“Fish are host to all sorts of parasites, you know.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“Mmm, roast fish.”
–Woodie, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“I'll be picking bones out of my teeth for weeks.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“Now where did the head go?”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“Thank yöu, fish, för the gift öf thy meat.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“It's not as cute anymore.”
–Webber, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“Delicious, delicious guilt.”
–Walani, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“Ah, I should have saved the head for soup!”
–Warly, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“Down th'hatch!”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“WILBA LIKETH THE FWOOSH'DED FISHY”
–Wilba, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“Glub Glub for belly”
–Wormwood, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
“It looks... edible?”
–Wheeler, when examining a Cooked Pierrot Fish.
A Cooked Pierrot Fish is a Pierrot Fish that has been cooked over a Campfire, Fire Pit, Chiminea or Star. It can also be used in various Crock Pot recipes. Cooked Pierrot Fish grants the same buffs as the uncooked variant.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Cá Pierrot là một Thức ăn cho Cá được giới thiệu trong DLC ​​Shipwrecked. Chúng có thể thu được bằng cách thả Trứng cá vào Trang trại Cá, cùng với Cá mú Tím, Cá Neon Quattro và Cá Nhiệt đới. Chúng có thể được sử dụng trong Nồi Hầm và được tính là 0,5 đơn vị Thịt và 1 đơn vị Cá. Họ được yêu cầu đặc biệt để nấu món Bouillabaisse nhiệt đới. Cá Pierrot không thể được sấy khô bằng Giá Phơi.

'''Pierrot Fish''' are a [[Fishes|Fish]] [[Food]] [[Item]] introduced in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC. They can be obtained by stocking [[Fish Farm]]s with [[Roe]], together with [[Purple Grouper]]s, [[Neon Quattro]] Fish and [[Tropical Fish]]. They can be used in the [[Crock Pot]], and count as 0.5 units of [[Meat]] and 1 unit of Fish. They are specifically required to cook the [[Tropical Bouillabaisse]]. Pierrot Fish cannot be dried using a [[Drying Rack]].

When consumed, Pierrot Fish grants the player a speed increase of 1 and greatly accelerates the rate at which their [[wetness]] decreases for 30 seconds. The speed bonus will stack with the bonus from [[Purple Grouper]]s and other sources, but will not stack with the bonus from [[Tropical Bouillabaisse]].

=={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia==
* Pierrot Fish were added to the ''Shipwrecked'' DLC more than a year after its official completion in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked#November 21st, 2017 - Home Sea Home Update|Home Sea Home]]'' update, after being tested in a beta branch for several months.
* The name is a reference to Pierrot, a stock character in ''commedia dell'arte'' - essentially a clown.  Most likely the appearance is based on a clownfish.
